hypothesis: If a graph neural network that concatenates frozen features from pre-trained single-gas etch-rate models with gas fractions is used to predict Ar/O2 mixture radial etching rates, then it will generalize with lower error to withheld high power/pressure conditions and to low-Ar-fraction regimes (xAr ≤ 0.2) than monolithic feedforward networks and standard GCN/GAT baselines because the single-gas feature embeddings encode transferable species-specific physics that enables compositional extrapolation.
